title: andropogon virginicus subsp. leucostachyus
intro: Andropogon virginicus subsp. leucostachyus, also known as broom sedge or leucostachy broom sedge, is a subspecies of grass native to North America. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat a variety of ailments. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, the root and rhizome of the plant are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
